_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage follows David Eck's Introduction to Programming Using Java. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the source guides curriculum order only.
title: Lambdas & Streams
summary: Describe a small action and apply it across collection data.
refresher: A lambda is a short function value, such as `name -> name.length()`. A stream processes collection data through steps like `filter`, `map`, and `sorted`, then finishes with an operation such as `toList`, `count`, or `forEach`. Stream steps do not change the original collection unless your code changes its objects.

blocks.body: Some collection work follows a clear sequence: keep certain items, change each one, then collect the results. Streams let the code state those steps in the same order.

blocks.caption: The filter keeps passing scores. The map adds five points to each remaining score. `toList` starts the work and gathers the results.

blocks.body: The expression before the arrow names each input. The expression after it produces a result. Braces are needed when the lambda body has more than one statement.

blocks.caption: A stream needs a final operation. Without `toList`, `count`, or another terminal step, the earlier steps describe work but do not run it.

blocks.title: A loop may be clearer
blocks.body: Use a regular loop when the work needs several changing variables, early exits, detailed error handling, or many side effects. Streams fit best when data moves through a short set of clear steps.

blocks.prompt: Which stream operation keeps only items that pass a condition?
blocks.why: Filter receives a condition and keeps values for which that condition is true. Map changes values instead of deciding whether to keep them.

blocks.body: Map changes every selected value into another value. The output type may differ from the input type, such as changing each String into an Integer length.

blocks.why: Map replaces each String with the Integer returned by `length()`. Ada has three characters and Grace has five.

blocks.title: Lambda and stream checklist
items: Use a lambda for one small action.
Read a stream from top to bottom as data steps.
Use filter to keep and map to change.
Choose a loop when the stream would hide the real work.
